name: Ventilation mode - Neurally adjusted (NAVA)
description: Neurally adjusted ventilatory assist (NAVA) — support driven by the patient's diaphragm electrical activity.

en.name: Ventilation mode - Neurally adjusted (NAVA)
en.shortDescription: Neurally adjusted ventilatory assist (NAVA) — support driven by the patient's diaphragm electrical activity.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Neurally Adjusted Ventilatory Assist (NAVA), a ventilation mode in which support is triggered and proportionally controlled by the electrical activity of the patient's diaphragm rather than by airway pressure or flow. In source data this is a **procedure** record indicating the ventilation mode in use; there is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. The mode is independent of whether ventilation is invasive (via an endotracheal/tracheostomy tube) or non-invasive (via a mask) — that distinction is captured in the Invasive mechanical ventilation and Non-invasive ventilation concept sets.

## Included Concepts

The set lists the SNOMED procedure concepts for these modes.

- *1243692 / 1297240007 — Neurally adjusted ventilatory assist (SNOMED)*

## Mapping Notes

- Map a recorded ventilation mode to the matching concept here based on the control variable / mode family, regardless of whether the patient is invasively or non-invasively ventilated.
- **Mode vs ventilation act**: this set captures the *mode* only; whether the patient is on invasive or non-invasive ventilation is recorded separately (Invasive mechanical ventilation / Non-invasive ventilation concept sets).
